steps.title: Know the condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the equations represent parallel lines: the variable coefficients are proportional, but the constants are not.

steps.title: Test choice D
steps.content: Multiply the first equation in choice D by $-2$: $$-2(-2x+3y)=-2(9)$$ $$4x-6y=-18$$ The second equation in choice D is $4x-6y=18$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D gives the same left side equal to two different constants, so the lines are parallel and never intersect. D is correct.
